papers.title_en: Modeling of a magnetic field sensor based on spin Hall magnetoresistance
papers.authors: Syeda Farwa Bukhari, Alessandro Magni, Witold Skowroński, Elena Losero, Vittorio Basso, Carlo Appino, Piotr Wiśniowski, Juergen Langer, Berthold Ocker, Dario Daghero, Michaela Kuepferling
papers.evidence: 与TMR对比及自旋电子传感器建模
papers.tldr: 本研究针对传统TMR传感器制造复杂及噪声高等问题，提出了一种基于自旋霍尔磁电阻（SMR）的磁场传感器建模与实验验证方案。通过结合Stoner-Wohlfarth模型、Fuchs-Sondheimer电流分布分析及改进的磁畴模型，研究了惠斯通桥配置下的SMR、AMR与SOT相互作用。实验验证了Pt/FeCoB和Ta/FeCoB双层膜器件，为优化SMR传感器灵敏度与功耗提供了关键设计准则。
papers.abstract_en: Next-generation spintronic sensors aim to overcome the limitations of traditional tunneling-magnetoresistance (TMR) devices, such as complex manufacturing, high $1/f$ noise, and significant offsets. This work presents a comprehensive modeling and experimental validation of a magnetic field sensor based on Spin Hall Magnetoresistance (SMR) in a Wheatstone bridge configuration. Utilizing a multiphysics approach, we simulate the interplay between SMR, Anisotropic Magnetoresistance (AMR), and Spin-Orbit Torque (SOT) using a Stoner-Wohlfarth model complemented by a Fuchs-Sondheimer analysis of current distribution. To account for the presence of magnetic domains, we incorporate a modified Stoner-Wohlfarth framework that considers non-uniform magnetization and domain wall motion through a "truncated astroid" approach, allowing for a statistical distribution of single-domain particles. The model is validated against experimental measurements of Pt/$\text{Fe}_{60}\text{Co}_{20}\text{B}_{20}$ and Ta/$\text{Fe}_{60}\text{Co}_{20}\text{B}_{20}$ bilayers patterned into Hall bars and Wheatstone bridges. The model provides critical design guidelines for optimizing material properties, layer thickness, and device layout to minimize power consumption and maximize sensitivity in SMR-based sensing applications.
